details: Starting SFPU kernel analysis for atanh operation

Found swish API header at tt_metal/hw/inc/api/compute/eltwise_unary/swish.h. swish_tile(idst) dispatches to llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_swish<APPROX>(idst). swish_tile_init() dispatches to llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_swish_init<APPROX>().
HARDTANH listed as parametrized type in hpp but no case in cpp get_op_init_and_func_parameterized. Searching broader codebase.

Read ckernel_sfpu_atanh.h for both WH and BH - identical implementations. Uses SFPI abstractions: dst_reg, vFloat, exexp, setexp, int32_to_float, vConst1, vConstFloatPrgm0-2. Cubic minimax polynomial approximation for ln(m) on [1,2). Formula: atanh(x) = 0.5 * (ln(1+x) - ln(1-x)).

_calculate_hardtanh_ exists in ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h (WH+BH) but NO higher-level API (hardtanh_tile), NO LLK dispatch, NO compute_kernel_api header. HARDTANH is listed as parametrized type but has no case in get_op_init_and_func. The SFPU kernel exists but the full dispatch chain is not wired up.

Traced full call chain: swish_tile(idst) -> llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_swish<APPROX>(idst) -> _llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_params_<APPROX>(calculate_swish<APPROX,ITERATIONS>, dst_index, RC) -> calculate_swish<false,8>(). SfpuType::swish uses default ADDR_MOD_7 (all zero increments). APPROXIMATION_MODE is unused in the kernel (no if constexpr branch on it).

TANHSHRINK uses REGISTER_UNARY_OPERATION which calls unary_impl -> UnaryProgramFactory. get_compute_kernel_path returns eltwise_sfpu.cpp for all ops (default case). But TANHSHRINK is NOT in get_op_init_and_func_default (would TT_THROW). tanhshrink_kernel.cpp exists but is not routed to. The op appears to be broken in the current codebase - it would throw at get_op_init_and_func_default. Analyzing the SFPU tanh implementation since tanhshrink = x - tanh(x) and the dedicated kernel uses tanh_tile + binary subtract.

key_findings: Uses SFPI abstractions: dst_reg, vFloat, exexp(SFPEXEXP), setexp(SFPSETEXP), int32_to_float(SFPCAST), vConstFloatPrgm0-2, vConst1. Cubic minimax polynomial for ln via Horner method (SFPMAD chain). WH and BH identical. ADDR_MOD_7 with all-zero increments.

Uses piecewise sigmoid approximation (polynomial + linear + saturation) with v_if/v_endif for conditional branches. WH and BH implementations are identical. APPROXIMATION_MODE template param is unused (no if constexpr branch on it).

details: This is a deep-nuked evaluation branch. ckernel_sfpu_tanh.h has been deleted from tt_llk and all ckernels directories. The LLK dispatch (llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_tanh) and core implementation (_calculate_tanh_) are missing. The compute kernel tanhshrink_kernel.cpp still exists but references the deleted tanh_tile() API. Additionally, get_compute_kernel_path has no routing case for TANHSHRINK - it falls through to eltwise_sfpu.cpp which would TT_THROW. The operation is non-functional on this branch.

key_findings: Uses exp_21f helper (Moroz 2022 exp2 algo), Taylor fallback for small |x|, SFPI abstractions (vFloat, v_if/v_endif, dst_reg), _float_to_int32_positive_ undefined dependency, WH and BH implementations identical

notes: HARDTANH is registered as parametrized type but dispatch chain is not wired: no case in get_op_init_and_func, no compute_kernel_api header, no LLK dispatch function, no SfpuType enum entry. Core SFPU kernel _calculate_hardtanh_ exists in ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h.

key_findings: WH and BH implementations identical. Uses s2vFloat16b for params, vFloat arithmetic, v_if/v_endif CC branches. Algebraic clamping via shifted additions rather than direct comparisons. 3 params: -neg_threshold, -(pos_threshold-neg_threshold), -pos_threshold.

details: TANHSHRINK has a dedicated compute kernel tanhshrink_kernel.cpp that does NOT use SFPU_OP_CHAIN dispatch. It directly calls tanh_tile(0) for SFPU tanh, then binary_dest_reuse_tiles<ELWSUB,DEST_TO_SRCB> for x - tanh(x). However, the ckernel_sfpu_tanh.h has been DELETED (deep-nuked). The LLK dispatch and core SFPU implementation are missing. Analysis will document the intended architecture and the SFPNONLINEAR hardware tanh instruction.

key_findings: ckernel_sfpu_tanh.h was deep-nuked. The dedicated compute kernel exists but references deleted LLK functions. tanh on WH/BH uses software implementation (not SFPNONLINEAR which is Quasar-only). The compute kernel combines SFPU tanh with FPU binary subtract to compute x-tanh(x).
